segments.text: You have seen this weird diamond on backpacks, but I guarantee you have no idea what it actually does. Most people think it's just a fashion statement, but it's actually a brilliant piece of
survival engineering. For years, people assumed this was just a brand logo. It just sits there, completely ignored by everyone. It's actually called a lash
tap. It was designed so that you can thread a strap through the slits to tie your extra gear, like wet shoes or a flashlight, to the outside of your back.
Still, why use thick leather or rubber? Why not use a normal fabric loop?
Because they were originally made for extreme mountain climbers. In freezing weather, a wet fabric loop turns into solid ice and snaps off. Leather and
rubber never freeze, keeping a survival gear perfectly safe. Genius design. Subscribe for more design secrets.
